meta.note: Bay Area seed compiled from public sources. Every record is flagged needs_verification: addresses, hours, and phone numbers MUST be confirmed with each provider before anyone relies on them. Coordinates are approximate.

services.name: St. Anthony's Dining Room
services.description: Free hot meals served daily, no questions asked.
services.address: 121 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102

services.name: GLIDE Daily Free Meals
services.description: Three free meals a day, 364 days a year. No ID, no cost, no questions.

services.name: LavaMaeˣ Mobile Showers
services.description: Free mobile showers and hygiene services. Schedule and locations vary by day.

services.name: Tenderloin Technology Lab
services.description: Free computer access and digital literacy education.
services.address: 121 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102

services.name: Hospitality House Shelter
services.description: Free emergency shelter and drop-in services for adults experiencing homelessness.

services.name: Homeless Advocacy Project
services.description: Free legal advice, advocacy, and services information for people who are homeless or low-income.

services.name: HealthRIGHT 360 Clinic
services.description: Free and low-cost integrated medical, dental, and behavioral health care. Sliding scale.

services.name: St. Vincent de Paul Free Dining Room
services.description: Free hot dining room for people who are homeless or low-income.

services.name: Alameda County Community Food Bank
services.description: Regional food bank; call the helpline to be connected to a nearby pantry or hot meal the same day.

services.name: CityTeam Oakland
services.description: Hot breakfast and dinner, clothing, and shelter services.

services.name: Dorothy Day House Drop-in Center
services.description: Daily drop-in: meals, showers, laundry, clothing, lockers, mail, internet, housing support. Open 365 days.

services.name: Women's Daytime Drop-in Center
services.description: Daytime drop-in and housing resources for women and families; food and hygiene services.

services.name: Berkeley Food & Housing Project
services.description: Emergency shelter, meals, and housing services. Call the shelter hotline for reservations.

services.name: Sacred Heart Community Service
services.description: Food pantry, clothes closet, and housing/benefits assistance. Must reside in Santa Clara County.

services.name: CityTeam San Jose
services.description: Daily hot meals, mobile food pantries, showers, clothing, and transitional housing.
services.address: 1174 Old Bayshore Hwy, San Jose, CA 95112
services.hours: Hot meals daily, call to confirm

services.name: LifeMoves Georgia Travis Center
services.description: Shelter and services for women and children experiencing homelessness.

services.name: Samaritan House
services.description: Free medical and dental clinics, food, shelter, and case management for San Mateo County.

services.name: LifeMoves Maple Street Shelter
services.description: Emergency shelter and supportive services for families and adults.

services.name: St. Vincent de Paul Free Dining Room (Marin)
services.description: Marin's primary free meals program. Open every day of the year; 500+ meals daily.

services.name: St. Vincent de Paul Housing Help Desk
services.description: Housing assessment, temporary shelter placement, and homelessness prevention. Located above the Free Dining Room.

services.name: Ritter Center
services.description: Marin's primary healthcare provider for people experiencing homelessness: medical, behavioral health, showers, food pantry.
services.verification_note: Ritter announced a move to 800 A St from 16 Ritter St -- confirm current location.

services.name: Homeward Bound of Marin - New Beginnings Center
services.description: Emergency shelter and services for adults, with job training via Fresh Starts Culinary Academy. In Novato.

services.name: Homeward Bound of Marin - Family Center
services.description: Emergency and transitional housing for families with children in Marin.

services.name: SF Rapid Response Hotline
services.organization: San Francisco Immigrant Legal & Education Network (SFILEN)
services.description: 24-hour hotline to report ICE enforcement, verify activity, and activate legal support. English, Spanish, Cantonese.
services.verification_note: Hotline number; not a walk-in address.

services.name: Marin Rapid Response Network
services.description: Marin County hotline to report and respond to immigration enforcement activity.

services.name: ACILEP Hotline (Alameda County)
services.organization: Alameda County Immigration Legal & Education Partnership
services.description: Rapid-response hotline for urgent ICE activity or detentions; mobilizes legal support and advocacy in Alameda County.

services.name: Centro Legal de la Raza
services.description: Free immigration legal services, removal/deportation defense, and know-your-rights presentations. Serving Oakland since 1969.

services.name: La Raza Centro Legal
services.description: Free immigration removal-defense legal services for Bay Area residents in removal proceedings; consultations for detained and non-detained people.

services.name: Bay Area Legal Aid
services.description: One of the largest legal-aid providers in the Bay Area (seven counties); immigration practice focused on humanitarian and vulnerable cases.

services.name: Jazzie's Place LGBT Shelter
services.description: 24-bed emergency shelter serving LGBT homeless adults, with sectioned areas for male, female, and gender-nonconforming guests. Part of the Dolores Street shelter.

services.name: SF LGBT Center Access Point
services.description: Coordinated-entry access point for LGBTQ youth (18-27) experiencing or facing homelessness: housing assessments, shelter connection, case management, drop-in.

services.name: Oakland LGBTQ Community Center
services.description: Resource hub connecting LGBTQ youth, adults, and seniors to emergency, shared, and permanent housing, plus health referrals and community services.

services.name: LGBTQ Youth Space
services.description: Drop-in center for LGBTQ+ youth and young adults 13-25 in Santa Clara County: peer counseling, support groups, case management, safer-sex supplies, psychiatry.

services.name: New Haven Inn (LGBTQ+ inclusive shelter)
services.description: Inclusive emergency shelter in downtown San Jose with focused support for people who identify as LGBTQ+. Location kept confidential for client safety; call or text to connect.
services.verification_note: Shelter location is confidential for safety; contact by phone/text.

services.name: SF Food Not Bombs (Wednesday)
services.description: Free hot vegetarian/vegan meals shared in public, no questions asked. Wednesday sharing at 16th & Mission BART Plaza.
services.address: 16th & Mission BART Plaza, San Francisco, CA 94103
services.verification_note: Volunteer-run; schedule/location shift. Confirm on sffnb.org before going.

services.name: SF Food Not Bombs (Friday)
services.description: Free hot vegetarian/vegan meals. Friday sharing near the SF Public Library at Larkin & Grove, across from City Hall.
services.address: Larkin St & Grove St, San Francisco, CA 94102
services.verification_note: Volunteer-run; schedule/location shift. Confirm on sffnb.org before going.

services.name: East Bay Food Not Bombs
services.description: Free hot vegan/vegetarian meals shared at People's Park, Berkeley, five days a week, plus rotating Oakland locations. Serving since 1991.
services.address: People's Park, 2556 Haste St, Berkeley, CA 94704
services.verification_note: Volunteer-run; Oakland days/locations rotate. Confirm on eastbayfoodnotbombs.org.

services.name: SF AIDS Foundation 6th Street Center
services.description: Syringe access & disposal, overdose prevention (naloxone, fentanyl test strips), hep C and HIV testing, substance-use counseling. Fixed site in Tenderloin/SoMa.

services.name: Santa Clara County Harm Reduction Program
services.organization: County of Santa Clara Public Health
services.description: Needs-based syringe distribution and disposal, sharps containers, naloxone, and rapid HIV/hep C/syphilis testing. No restrictions.
services.hours: See schedule; arrive 30 min before close for testing
services.verification_note: Multiple mobile sites; confirm current schedule with the program.

services.name: HEPPAC Syringe Access
services.organization: HIV Education & Prevention Project of Alameda County
services.description: Syringe exchange, naloxone (OPEND), HIV/hep C testing, and a drop-in center (showers, laundry, food). Fixed sites + mobile across Oakland and Contra Costa.
services.address: Fruitvale, E 12th St & 23rd Ave, Oakland, CA 94606
services.hours: Tue 6-8pm Fruitvale; call for other sites
services.verification_note: Multiple fixed + mobile sites with different hours; confirm current schedule.

services.name: Berkeley NEED
services.description: Volunteer-run needle exchange and naloxone distribution. Look for the black van near the Berkeley Free Clinic; anonymous, no dirty-needle requirement.
services.verification_note: Van-based, rotating weekly sites; confirm days/times on berkeleyneed.org.

services.name: Punks With Lunch Oakland
services.description: Volunteer mutual-aid group providing harm-reduction supplies, naloxone, and sack lunches via outreach in West Oakland and beyond.
services.verification_note: Street outreach; no fixed address. Confirm schedule on their site/Instagram.

services.name: Spiral Gardens
services.organization: Spiral Gardens Community Food Security Project
services.description: Community farm where volunteers grow organic food together; the harvest is shared among volunteers and given free to people who need it. Plant nursery on site.

services.name: Alemany Farm
services.description: San Francisco's largest urban farm (3.5 acres). Free produce and donation-based workshops; no one turned away for lack of funds. Volunteer workdays open to all.

services.name: Homies Empowerment Freedom Farm
services.description: Community-led East Oakland farm that gives away free food and teaches people how to grow it cheaply. 28 open garden boxes; soil, seeds, and water provided.

services.name: Long Haul Infoshop
services.description: All-volunteer anarchist resource center: radical lending library, zine-making space, free internet, reading room, and meeting space. Free literature.

services.name: Omni Commons
services.description: Collectively-run community commons housing nine collectives — hackerspace, biolab, free spaces, and event halls — run on consensus and radical inclusion.

services.name: Mandela Grocery Cooperative
services.description: Worker-owned cooperative grocery in West Oakland focused on affordable, healthy food access in a historically underserved neighborhood.
